data.title: Silver surges to new record as strong ETF inflows add to momentum
data.description:
Silver futures hit another record high Friday and logged a fourth weekly gain out of five, as strong inflows to ETFs sustain the metal's sizzling rally; gold finished roughly flat for the day and the week.

Total additions to silver-backed ETFs in the four days through Thursday already reached the highest for any full week since July, Bloomberg reported, a strong indicator of investor demand.

Silver has roughly doubled this year, with the rally accelerating in the last two months due in part to a historic squeeze in London, which has eased in recent weeks as more metal was shipped to the world's biggest silver trading hub, but other markets such as China are now seeing supply constraints.

Silver is "following the pathway of gold and many investors still believe that silver is quite cheap in relative terms," TD Securities global head of commodity strategy Bart Melek wrote, pointing to structural deficits and rising demand for electrification as supportive factors.

The metal could climb to $62/oz in the next three months "on the back of Fed cuts, robust investment demand, and physical deficit," Citi analysts said in a note.

Like gold, silver's recent surge also has been supported by rising expectations that the U.S. Federal Reserve will reduce interest rates at its meeting next week.

On Friday, the core Personal Consumption Expenditures price index - the Federal Reserve's preferred metric for measuring inflation - stayed under 3% in September, showing a moderate month-to-month rise in prices that is unlikely to prevent policymakers from considering an interest rate cut at the Fed's December 9-10 meeting.

The CME's FedWatch tool indicates an 87.2% probability of a 25-basis-point rate cut at next week's meeting.

This week, front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for December delivery ended -0.1% to $4,212.90/oz, down two of the last three weeks, while front-month Comex December silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +3.5% to a new record-high settlement of $58.422/oz; on Friday, gold was flat, and silver gained 2.7%.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Kymera rises on upcoming data release for eczema asset KT-621
data.description:
• Kymera Therapeutics (KYMR) is up ~9% in after-hours trading Friday after announcing it will release phase 1b data on its atopic dermatitis (eczema) candidate KT-621 on Dec. 8.
  • A press release about the results will be issued at 7a ET that day, followed by a webcast at 8a. 
  • KT-621 is known as an oral STAT6 degrader. STAT6, according to Kymera, is "an essential transcription factor in the IL-4/IL-13 signaling pathways and the central driver of Th2 inflammation in allergic diseases"
  • Other potential indications for the asset include asthma and COPD.

data.title: JNJ releases new data bolstering Inlexzo for bladder cancer
data.description:
• A phase 2 study of Johnson & Johnson's Inlexzo (gemcitabine intravesical system) found strong survival results after one year in patients with Bacillus Calmette-Guérin ("BCG")-unresponsive, high-risk, papillary-only non-muscle invasive bladder cancer.
  • Data from a cohort of the SunRISe-1 study found that at a median follow-up of 15.9 months, the one-year disease-free survival rate was 74.3%. At one year, progression-free survival was 95.6%, and overall survival was 98%.
  • Also, 92.3% of participants did not require radical cystectomy.

data.title: Diamond Estates acquires Perigon Beverage
data.description:
• Diamond Estates Wines & Spirits (DWS:CA)  announced on Friday that the company issued 0.68M common shares at an issuance price of $0.21 per share as part of the purchase price of the acquisition of Perigon Beverage.
  • In connection with the acquisition, the company issued 0.25M common shares to the principals of 2RL Capital at a deemed issuance price of $0.196 per common share, pursuant to the engagement letter.

data.title: Fly-E group announces receipt of delinquency notice from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Fly-E Group (FLYE) announced on Friday that the company received a written notice from Nasdaq, notifying the company that it currently does not satisfy Listing Rule, as a result of not having timely filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission its Form 10-Q for the period ended September 30, 2025.
  • The Notice is a notification of deficiency, not of imminent delisting, and has no immediate effect on the listing or trading of the company’s securities on Nasdaq.
  • The company has 60 calendar days from the date of the notice, or until January 26, 2026, to submit to Nasdaq a plan to regain compliance with the Nasdaq Listing Rule. If the company submits a plan to Nasdaq and Nasdaq accepts the plan, Nasdaq can grant an exception to up to 180 calendar days from the due date of the filing of the Form 10-Q, or until May 20, 2026, to regain compliance. 
  • FLYE -8.34% after hours to $7.58.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rocket Doctor appoints Christopher Cherry as interim CFO
data.description:
• Rocket Doctor AI (OTCQB:AIRDF)on Friday announced that Christopher Cherry shall assume the role of interim Chief Financial Officer, to replace David Worner, effective December 8, 2025.
  • Cherry is currently the company’s Chair of the Audit Committee.
  • Additionally, the company has granted an aggregate 579,028 stock options, exercisable at $0.84 per share and valid for a term of three or five years, and an aggregate 931,112 restricted share units, valid for a term between two to five years, to executive officers, directors and consultants of the company and its subsidiary, Rocket Doctor Inc.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Clearmind Medicine announces receipt of Nasdaq minimum bid price notification
data.description:
• Clearmind Medicine (CMND) on Friday said it received a notice from Nasdaq indicating its common shares have traded below $1.00 for the last 30 consecutive business days, putting it out of compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2).
  • The company has 180 days, until June 2, 2026, to regain compliance by maintaining a closing bid price of at least $1.00 for 10 consecutive business days.
  • Shares will continue trading on Nasdaq under the symbol “CMND” during the compliance period.
  • CMND -1.70% after hours to $0.1269.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GSK ends collaboration with IDEAYA for two candidates
data.description:
• GSK (GSK) is terminating a collaboration and licensing agreement with IDEAYA Biosciences (IDYA) for two of the latter's assets.
  • The 2020 pact covers the Werner Helicase (IDE275) and Pol Theta (IDE705) clinical programs, both under investigation to treat solid tumors.
  • The two programs utilize synthetic lethality, a relatively new field in oncology treatments involving genetic interaction where the combination of two genetic events results in cell death or death of an organism.
  • The agreement called for IDEAYA to receive US profit share and ex-US royalties for both candidates. IDEAYA also received a $100M upfront payment.

data.title: LibertyStream launches up to $10M non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• LibertyStream (LIB:CA) (TSXV: LIB | OTCQB: VLTLF | FSE: I2D) on Friday said it is offering a non-brokered private placement of units at $0.65 each, aiming to raise up to $10 million.
  • Each unit includes one common share and one warrant to purchase a common share at $1.00 over 36 months.
  • Proceeds will be used to develop the company’s direct lithium extraction technology, scale up lithium carbonate production in Texas, provide product samples to potential customers, and fund general working capital.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 10, 2025, subject to customary conditions and regulatory approvals.
  • VLTLF closed -1.24% at $0.5649.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Constellation Energy reaches deal with DoJ for Calpine acquisition
data.description:
Constellation Energy (CEG) closed -2.4% on Friday after saying it reached a resolution with the U.S. Department of Justice on the conditions required to complete its proposed $16.4 billion acquisition of Calpine, marking the final regulatory clearance needed to complete the transaction.

In July, the companies offered to divest four of Calpine's power plants in Pennsylvania and Delaware - Hay Road, Edge Moor, Bethlehem, and York 1 - as part of a separate agreement with the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.

As part of the DoJ resolution, Constellation (CEG) also agreed to divest the 828 MW York 2 natural gas-fired plant in Pennsylvania, the 605 MW gas-fired Jack Fusco Energy Center near Houston, and a minority ownership interest in the 385 MW gas-fired Gregory Power Plant near Corpus Christi, Texas.

The DoJ said the agreement addresses concerns that the acquisition could harm competition and increase prices for consumers in the ERCOT and PJM Interconnection grids.
